title: Sonar transducer vendor change: DepthWave → AquaSound Corp (P-1148 Rev B)
text:
Engineering Change Request ECR-218.

Raised by: Cass Doyle (Payload Systems Engineer), 2024-09-05.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2024-09-12.

Summary: Change sonar transducer (P-1148) from DepthWave Systems to AquaSound Corp.

Rationale: Integration testing (KES-156) revealed unacceptable frequency drift in the DepthWave transducer (KES-158): the centre frequency shifted from specified 200kHz to 184kHz over a 2-hour thermal soak at 35°C — a 16kHz drift. The sonar signal processor (P-1147) DSP filter and the sonar driver firmware (SRC-011) are tuned to 200kHz ±2kHz. A 16kHz drift cannot be compensated in software without a full DSP filter redesign and re-qualification.

Vendor evaluation at WIKI-045 (Sonar Vendor Selection Meeting, 2024-08-29) compared DepthWave, AquaSound Corp, and MarineSpec Ltd. AquaSound Corp was selected on:
- Frequency stability: ±1.5kHz over temperature (vs ±16kHz for DepthWave)
- Extended bandwidth: 100–400kHz (enables future multi-frequency mode)
- Depth rating: 30m (vs 20m for DepthWave)
- DNV-GL certification data available (reduces qualification test burden)
- IP68 connector standard on P-1148 Rev B (DepthWave required custom cable)

O-ring flange compatibility with P-1149 mounting bracket (DRW-517) confirmed at WIKI-045.

Impact:
- P-1148: Revised to Rev B (AquaSound Corp supplier, updated interface drawing)
- P-1149: Sonar mounting bracket — compatible, no change (O-ring confirmed)
- DOC-462: Sonar System ICD to be updated for AquaSound electrical and acoustic interface
- SRC-011 (sonar_driver.c): DSP filter centre frequency remains 200kHz, but anti-alias filter width can be narrowed — minor update
- DOC-468: Sonar Performance Test Report to be re-issued with AquaSound data
